content:
- All required gates passed for task 02: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.
- Marked the task file done with passes=true and ran .ralph/task_switch.sh successfully, which updated the Ralph task-tracking files.
- Next is final repo bookkeeping: inspect git status, commit all changed files with the required task-finished message including gate evidence, then push.

- Staged all Ralph/task metadata, committed with the required task-finished message, and pushed master successfully.
- Post-commit docs publish hook ran and completed cleanly; no extra tracked changes were left behind.
- Worktree sanity check next, then stop immediately.

- Read progress log output and task summaries from .ralph/current_tasks.md plus completed-task state from .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Confirmed active bug tasks are absent and all recorded bug tasks in done summary currently have <passes>true</passes>, so selection has moved to non-bug active tasks.
- Next I am resolving blockers for the authoritative-managed-postgres-config tasks and scanning active task files for priority/chain signals before writing .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Resolved active-task precedence: no unblocked failing bug tasks exist, so selection moved to other unblocked failing tasks.
- Verified authoritative-managed-postgres-config task 03 is unblocked because backup-removal tasks 01/02/04/05 and authoritative tasks 01/02 all already have <passes>true</passes> in .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Verified sibling tasks 04 and 05 remain blocked on task 03, and no higher-priority active task outranks it. Wrote .ralph/tasks/story-authoritative-managed-postgres-config/03-task-take-full-ownership-of-replica-recovery-signal-and-auto-conf-state.md to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Read previous Ralph progress and current task pointer.
- Opened task 03 for authoritative managed postgres config ownership; it is still not_started and does not yet contain a plan marker.
- Next I need to inspect the named Rust modules and tests just enough to write a detailed plan into the task file, then append TO BE VERIFIED and stop immediately.

- Inspected the concrete code paths named by task 03: process worker basebackup args, managed postgres config materialization, startup planning, HA process dispatch, real-binary pginfo tests, and lifecycle docs.
- Confirmed there is currently no postgresq.auto.conf/recovery.signal ownership in the codebase; standby.signal is managed but resume logic still partially trusts on-disk leftovers.
- Confirmed typed-config prerequisites are done and the backup-removal prerequisites are done too, but their blocked_by paths in this task header still point at the wrong story directory; I should mention that in the task plan. Next is patching the task file with the full execution plan plus TO BE VERIFIED, then stop immediately.

- Wrote the full execution plan into task 03 and appended TO BE VERIFIED exactly as requested.
- The plan now calls out the real implementation surface: postgres_managed_conf.rs, postgres_managed.rs, process/worker.rs, runtime/node.rs, ha/process_dispatch.rs, real-binary tests, and lifecycle docs; it also notes the mispointed blocked_by paths.
- Stop now. The next engineer/cycle should read the task file, perform a deep skeptical review that changes at least one thing, replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE only once satisfied, and only then execute.

- Read prior progress and current Ralph task pointer.
- Found this task is already at the TO BE VERIFIED checkpoint from the previous cycle, so this turn is a skeptical plan review rather than first-plan authoring.
- Next I need to read the task file, inspect whether the plan is actually complete and sound, change at least one substantive thing in the plan, and only then switch the marker to NOW EXECUTE if it survives review.

- Reviewed task 03 plan skeptically against the actual code and found concrete gaps: the exhaustive checklist omitted src/postgres_managed_conf.rs, the auto-conf policy was still framed as a choice instead of a fixed contract, and runtime/HA still had duplicated managed-state parsing semantics.
- Patched the task file to fix those issues, expanded docs scope, mandated deterministic auto-conf quarantine, added a shared managed-state helper step, and replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE.
- Execution phase has started. First code change block is the managed typing/materialization layer in postgres_managed_conf.rs and postgres_managed.rs, then I will wire runtime/HA to the shared helper and remove pg_basebackup side effects.

- Implemented the first execution block in code: managed start intent now explicitly represents Primary/Replica/Recovery, recovery-related extra_guc keys are reserved, managed signal-file materialization is typed, and postgresql.auto.conf is deterministically quarantined to pgtm.unmanaged.postgresql.auto.conf.
- Added a shared read_existing_replica_start_intent helper in src/postgres_managed.rs so runtime and HA can reuse one managed-state parser instead of carrying separate ad hoc parsing.
- Focused compile/test slice passed with cargo test postgres_managed --lib. Next I need to wire runtime/node.rs and ha/process_dispatch.rs to the shared helper and remove any remaining stale assumptions such as signal-presence-as-authority and pg_basebackup side effects.

- Runtime startup planning now uses the shared managed-state reader instead of raw standby.signal presence, and HA process dispatch reuses the same helper instead of its own parser.
- Removed the only remaining pg_basebackup -R use from src/pginfo/worker.rs by manually creating standby.signal plus primary_conninfo in the real test setup.
- Added new evidence: runtime tests proving DCS beats stale local auto_conf, and a real PostgreSQL clone/start test proving managed startup quarantines postgresql.auto.conf, replaces stale recovery.signal with standby.signal, and successfully starts on the managed port. Docs for lifecycle bootstrap/recovery, operator configuration, and contributor HA pipeline were updated. Next is the full make check/test/test-long/lint sequence and then task bookkeeping.
